summary: Iceberg Quantum, founded by Felix Thomsen, Larry Cohen, and Sam Smith, has announced Pinnacle, a fault-tolerant quantum computing architecture based on quantum low-density parity-check codes. Validated by numerical simulations, Pinnacle claims to reduce the qubit overhead required to break RSA-2048 encryption from millions to under 100,000, resetting expectations for when cryptographically relevant quantum computing may emerge. Iceberg Quantum is working with leading hardware companies including PsiQuantum, Diraq, and IonQ, who aim to reach these scales within three to five years. The company closed a $6 million seed round led by LocalGlobe, with Blackbird and DCVC, to expand research, intellectual property, and partnerships. This approach is positioned to accelerate practical fault-tolerant quantum systems.
